guardrail_evaluations.explanation: The effective discount of 33.3% exceeds the mid-market soft ceiling of 20%. This is a yellow flag — the AE should be prepared to justify the competitive context in any approval conversation.
At 33.3% discount, this deal exceeds the 30% mid-market Finance sign-off threshold and cannot proceed without explicit Finance approval. The deal must be re-routed before closing.

The ramp schedule spans 12 months (months 1–12), far exceeding the 3-month mid-market limit. This hides significant discount in the ramp structure and triggers mandatory Finance approval for ASC 606 and revenue recognition reasons.
Estimated gross margin of ~10% is well below the 40% warn threshold. Even under conservative assumptions, this deal is a major margin outlier — concessions need to be restructured.
At ~10% estimated gross margin, this deal violates the absolute 25% floor and cannot be approved in its current form. All concessions must be reshaped before re-quoting — this is a hard block.

alternative_structures.label: Option A: 36-Month Commit + Reduced Discount, Drop Free Period
alternative_structures.description: Extend term to 36 months at a 22% list discount ($280,800 ACV). Eliminate the 6-month free period entirely — replace it with a 90-day onboarding credit ($18K one-time) applied to invoice. Remove the 90-day termination-for-convenience clause. Lock expansion pricing through 2027 as requested. Implementation services waived as goodwill given 3-year commit.
alternative_structures.rationale: The 6-month free period is the single biggest margin killer — it effectively creates a 50%+ discount in Year 1. A 36-month commit justifies a 22% discount (still above the soft ceiling but defensible with Finance), brings margin back to list-equivalent level, and gives Tessera the consolidation story and pricing certainty they want. The AE trades the out clause for term security.

alternative_structures.label: Option B: Ramp-to-Full with Year-2 Escalator, No Free Period
alternative_structures.description: Structured as a 24-month deal at $260,000 ACV (28% discount). Replace the 6-month free period with a true ramp: months 1–3 at 50% of ACV ($130K annualized), months 4–6 at 75% ($195K annualized), months 7–24 at full ACV ($260K). Year 2 includes a built-in 8% escalator ($280,800). Remove the 90-day out clause. Implementation services waived. Expansion pricing locked through 2027.
alternative_structures.rationale: This mirrors how Tessera's RevOps team will actually ramp users post-consolidation — a phased ramp is honest and reduces sticker shock, while the Year-2 escalator builds in revenue recovery. Margin is above the absolute floor, discount is at the block-without-approval threshold (requires Finance sign-off but is approvable), and the escalator partially compensates for Year-1 ramp. Eliminates the 90-day out clause risk in exchange for the slower ramp.

alternative_structures.label: Option C: Professional Services as Separate Line + Smaller Subscription Discount
alternative_structures.description: Re-bundle implementation services as a paid line item at $30K (25% reduction from $40K list as a goodwill gesture). Subscription discount reduced to 20% ($288,000 ACV). Free period compressed to 60 days (not 6 months). Retain expansion pricing lock through 2027. Remove 90-day out clause entirely — offer instead a 12-month early termination fee equal to 3 months ACV as a compromise. Total Year-1 revenue: $318,000 ($288K subscription + $30K PS).
alternative_structures.rationale: Unbundling PS restores a full-margin revenue line that the current proposal gives away entirely. Bringing the subscription discount to exactly the soft ceiling means it passes the warn guardrail and only requires Finance sign-off (not a hard block). The 90-day out clause is replaced with a mutual off-ramp that still protects Clay's revenue. Total deal economics improve significantly with PS revenue included.

pricing.reasoning_summary: This deal violates every active guardrail simultaneously — the 33.3% subscription discount clears both the mid-market warn (20%) and block-without-approval (30%) thresholds, the 12-month ramp far exceeds the 3-month mid-market sign-off limit, and estimated gross margin of ~10% breaches the absolute floor of 25%, making this a hard block in current form. The margin estimate uses the working assumption that list price carries a 40% gross margin (i.e., COGS = 60% × $360K = $216K), so the CFO should refine this with actual COGS before relying on the specific margin percentage. The non-standard clauses compound the economic risk: the 6-month free period effectively doubles the implicit discount in Year 1, the waived $40K implementation adds further margin erosion, and the 90-day termination-for-convenience right creates material churn exposure on a deal that requires 24 months to generate adequate return. None of the three precedent deals were directly applicable (all enterprise-segment, lower discount rates), so alternative structures were built from guardrail logic and deal mechanics rather than precedent.

performance_obligations.name: Platform subscription (paid term)
performance_obligations.description: SaaS subscription for sales engagement/data platform over the 18-month paid term (months 7-24).
performance_obligations.rationale: Stand-ready obligation to provide continuous access to the platform; customer simultaneously receives and consumes benefits ratably (ASC 606-10-25-27).

performance_obligations.name: Free-period platform access (months 1-6)
performance_obligations.description: Six months of free platform access prior to the paid term commencing in month 7.
performance_obligations.rationale: Not a separate PO — the free period is a material right/discount embedded in the same subscription service. The total $480K TCV should be recognized ratably across the entire 24-month service period.

performance_obligations.name: Implementation services (waived)
performance_obligations.description: Onboarding/implementation services normally priced at $40K, contractually waived.
performance_obligations.rationale: Implementation is capable of being distinct, but since it is waived (zero consideration allocated) it produces no revenue; if not distinct from the SaaS, its cost is bundled into ratable subscription revenue.

variable_consideration_flags.explanation: The 90-day anytime out clause may shorten the enforceable contract term under ASC 606-10-25-3. If deemed substantive, the contract term could be as short as 90 days, requiring revenue to be recognized over a much shorter horizon and treating each renewal period as a contract modification.

Locking 2026 pricing for seat expansions through 2027 likely constitutes a material right (ASC 606-10-55-41). A portion of transaction price may need to be allocated to this option based on expected expansion volume and discount vs. standalone selling price.
The 6-month free period is a discount embedded in the subscription; the $480K TCV is allocated ratably across the full 24-month service period rather than only the 18 paid months.
Waived $40K implementation services represent additional consideration concession; if implementation is distinct, SSP allocation may shift transaction price between POs.

contract_modification_risk.explanation: The 90-day TFC clause and the 2026 expansion pricing lock through 2027 both create scenarios where future events (early termination, seat additions at locked pricing) would trigger reassessment of contract term, transaction price, and allocation under ASC 606-10-25-10 through 25-13.

recognized_revenue_schedule.notes: Ratable recognition of total $480K TCV over full 24-month service period = $20K/month, even during free months.
$20K/month ratable; cash billed $20K/month per ramp schedule.
$20K/month ratable; assumes no exercise of TFC clause.

red_flags.label: 90-day termination-for-convenience clause
red_flags.explanation: An anytime 90-day TFC right may collapse the enforceable contract term to 90 days under ASC 606, materially changing revenue recognition and undermining the 24-month TCV. Requires CFO/auditor pre-clearance.

red_flags.label: Expansion pricing lock through 2027 (material right)
red_flags.explanation: Locked 2026 pricing for future seat expansions likely creates a material right requiring transaction price allocation; could defer revenue and complicate downstream modification accounting.

red_flags.label: 6 months free + waived $40K implementation
red_flags.explanation: Combined concessions exceed 50% of ACV. Must be reflected as discount in transaction price allocation across the 24-month service period, not booked as Year 2 catch-up.

red_flags.label: 35% discount vs. list
red_flags.explanation: Steep discount may distort SSP analysis and affect future deal benchmarking; ensure SSP is established independent of this contract.

red_flags.label: Annual upfront billing with deferred service start
red_flags.explanation: Cash collected upfront while service is delivered ratably creates a sizable contract liability; ensure deferred revenue waterfall is modeled correctly.

asc606.reasoning_summary: This deal combines multiple interacting variable-consideration sources (TFC, expansion pricing lock, free period, waived implementation) that materially complicate ASC 606 treatment. The 90-day TFC right is the dominant issue — it may shorten the enforceable term and invalidate ratable 24-month recognition. Transaction price ($480K) should be spread ratably across the full service period, but a material right allocation for the 2027 expansion lock is likely required. This is an automated estimate; final determination requires CFO/auditor review.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Customer requests a 90-day termination-for-convenience right exercisable at any time during the 24-month term, with no cause required.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: A 90-day anytime TFC on a 24-month deal means Tessera can walk after month 7 (first paid month) with just 90 days' notice, exposing Clay to as little as ~$60K in collected revenue against a 35%-discounted price, waived $40K implementation, and 6 months of free access — making the effective payback period unachievable. At a health score of 58, churn risk is real, not theoretical.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Customer may terminate this Agreement for convenience no earlier than the end of month eighteen (18) of the Initial Term, upon not less than ninety (90) days' prior written notice to Clay. In the event of such termination, Customer shall pay all fees due through the effective termination date plus a wind-down fee equal to three (3) months of the then-current monthly subscription fee. For the avoidance of doubt, no termination-for-convenience right shall be available during the first eighteen (18) months of the Initial Term.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept a TFC window opening at month 15 (not 18), with 90-day notice and a two-month wind-down fee in lieu of three, provided all other concessions (6-month free period, waived impl.) remain unchanged.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has accepted TFC rights with a minimum committed period floor (typically 12–18 months) on deals above $200K ACV; unconditional anytime TFC has not been approved at this discount depth.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Customer requests that any seat or usage expansions purchased through end of 2027 be billed at 2026 contracted per-unit rates, regardless of standard price increases.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: A pricing lock through end of 2027 effectively converts a 24-month deal into a price-protected 30-month exposure (deal closes May 2026, lock runs to Dec 2027). If Clay raises list prices — standard 5–10% annually — and Tessera expands significantly, Clay absorbs the delta on every incremental seat. Combined with the 35% existing discount, expansion units could be priced at 65% of a stale list, compounding margin erosion on what should be the highest-margin revenue in the relationship.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Expansion seats purchased during the Initial Term shall be priced at Customer's contracted per-unit rate as of the Effective Date, subject to an annual uplift of no more than five percent (5%) applied on each anniversary of the Effective Date. This pricing protection expires upon the conclusion of the Initial Term and does not apply to any Renewal Term or to new product SKUs not included in the original Order Form.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept flat 2026 pricing for expansions through end of the initial 24-month term only (i.e., through May 2028, not an artificial Dec 2027 cutoff), with no annual uplift, but only if TFC is restructured per the counter above.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has offered within-term expansion price protection with a 5% annual uplift cap on strategic enterprise deals; a hard calendar-date lock decoupled from the contract anniversary has not been approved.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Six months of free access (months 1–6) before the paid term begins at month 7, effectively deferring all revenue collection to the back 18 months of the 24-month contract.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Combined with the 90-day TFC, the free period creates a scenario where Tessera receives $120K in free platform value and $40K in waived services, then exits at month 9 (first paid month + 90-day notice) having paid only ~$60K — a net loss position for Clay. Even without early exit, Clay collects $240K TCV across only 18 paid months, meaning any churn or non-renewal in month 19 leaves Clay under-recovering against cost-to-serve and acquisition cost on a 35%-discounted deal.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: The free onboarding period shall be limited to four (4) months (months 1–4). The paid term shall commence on the first day of month five (5). The ramp schedule shall be adjusted accordingly so that full contracted monthly fees are due no later than month nine (9). Clay will credit Customer the value of any unused implementation services (up to $20,000) as a fee credit applied against months 5–6 invoices in lieu of a full services waiver.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept the 6-month free period only if (a) TFC is restructured to open no earlier than month 18, and (b) the expansion pricing lock includes the 5% annual uplift cap. If all three concessions stack without modification, the deal cannot ship.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has approved free periods up to 3 months on competitive displacement deals at comparable ACV; 6-month free periods have only been approved when paired with multi-year hard commits (no TFC).

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Customer requests full waiver of implementation services, normally valued at $40,000, as part of the deal package.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Waiving $40K in services on a $240K ACV deal represents an additional ~17% effective discount on top of the stated 35%, bringing the blended discount versus list to approximately 47%. More critically, without paid implementation, Tessera's time-to-value is at risk, which — given a health score of 58 at signing — increases the probability of early TFC invocation.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Clay shall provide implementation services at no additional charge up to a value of twenty thousand dollars ($20,000) ('Included Implementation Credit'). Any implementation services required beyond the Included Implementation Credit shall be scoped and priced separately. The Included Implementation Credit is non-transferable and expires ninety (90) days after the Effective Date.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept full $40K services waiver only if the free period is reduced to 4 months and the TFC floor is set at month 15 or later. Do not accept full waiver alongside an unrestricted TFC.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Partial services credits ($15K–$25K) have been approved on competitive displacements; full $40K+ waivers typically require VP of Sales sign-off and are rare below $300K ACV.

standard_clauses_affirmed: Contract term: 24 months (standard for mid-market displacement)
redline.one_line_summary: This deal cannot ship as-is — the combination of a 6-month free period, unrestricted 90-day TFC, and expansion pricing lock creates a scenario where Clay takes on all delivery risk with no minimum revenue guarantee; we need the TFC floored at month 18, the free period cut to 4 months, and the expansion lock capped with a 5% annual uplift before this goes to legal.
redline.reasoning_summary: The headline risk is the compounding of four concessions that individually are each manageable but together eliminate Clay's downside protection entirely: a 35% discount, 6 months free, $40K waived services, and a 90-day anytime TFC mean Tessera could exit after paying as little as ~$60K against ~$160K in value already delivered. The expansion pricing lock adds a secondary margin risk on what should be the deal's upside. Tessera's health score of 58 and a 10-day bake-off window suggest urgency on their side but also instability — the TFC is the single must-fix clause before this deal can proceed. Customer signals from Exa are largely noise (different Tessera entities), so competitive leverage assessment relies entirely on AE-reported bake-off dynamics.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Standard sub-$100K with ≤15% discount → AE Manager
required_approvers.rationale: Default rule applies to all deals; AE Manager Marcus Reilly is always the first approver.
Deal ACV of $240K falls within the $100K–$500K range requiring RevOps oversight for structure consistency.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Discount over 25% routes to CFO + TCV over $1M requires CFO + Pricing block_without_approval guardrails
required_approvers.rationale: Effective discount of 33.3% exceeds the 25% threshold; TCV of $480K does not trigger the >$1M rule but the 33.3% discount and pricing guardrail blocks (mid-market 30% threshold, ramp length exceeding 3-month limit, gross margin floor breach) require explicit CFO approval. Pricing Agent flagged block_without_approval severity on discount (33.3% vs. 30% threshold) and ramp length (12 months vs. 3-month limit). ASC 606 flagged two block_without_approval red flags: 90-day TFC may collapse contract term and expansion pricing lock creates material right allocation complexity.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Non-standard clauses: termination_for_convenience, expansion_pricing_lock, free_period, implementation_services_waived + Redline Agent overall_redline_priority = block
required_approvers.rationale: Deal contains four flagged non-standard clauses. Redline Agent assigned overall priority 'block' — the 90-day anytime TFC, 6-month free period, expansion pricing lock through 2027, and $40K waived implementation combine to eliminate downside protection. Legal must negotiate TFC floor (month 18 minimum), free period reduction (4 months max), expansion lock cap (5% annual uplift), and/or services credit restructuring before the deal can proceed.

blockers_to_address_first: Pricing guardrail block_absolute: gross margin estimated at ~10% violates 25% floor. Pricing Agent provided three alternative deal structures (Option A: 36-month at 22% discount with margin ~40%; Option B: 24-month ramp-to-full at 28% discount with margin ~30%; Option C: unbundle $30K PS at 20% subscription discount with margin ~39.2%). AE must restructure the deal economics before it can ship.

Redline block priority: 90-day anytime termination-for-convenience clause must be renegotiated. Minimum fallback: TFC window opens at month 15 (not immediately), with 90-day notice and 2-month wind-down fee. Suggested counter: TFC not available until month 18, with 3-month wind-down fee.
Redline block priority: 6-month free period combined with TFC creates churn exposure. Must reduce to 4 months or restructure as a fee credit against months 5–6 in exchange for TFC floor at month 18.

Redline block priority: Expansion pricing lock through 2027 creates material right allocation complexity (ASC 606 block_without_approval red flag). Must add 5% annual uplift cap and/or scope to the initial 24-month term only (not artificial Dec 2027 cutoff).

ASC 606 block_without_approval: 90-day TFC may collapse enforceable contract term to 90 days under ASC 606, invalidating 24-month ratable recognition. CFO and auditor must pre-clear TFC language before execution.

ASC 606 block_without_approval: Expansion pricing lock through 2027 likely constitutes a material right requiring transaction price allocation and may defer revenue. CFO/auditor assessment of allocation methodology required.

Missing Pricing Exception Form: Deal violates multiple guardrails (discount >30% threshold, ramp >3 months, margin <25% absolute floor). AE must submit completed pricing exception form with competitive context, customer health score assessment, and likely restructure per Pricing Agent recommendations.
approval.one_line_summary: This deal routes to AE Manager → RevOps + CFO + Legal (parallel) with an expected 7 business days; it cannot proceed without restructuring: the Pricing Agent's guardrail block (10% margin vs. 25% floor) requires a deal rethink (shorter term, lower discount, or unbundled services), and Legal's block-priority redlines (90-day anytime TFC + 6-month free period + expansion lock) must be renegotiated to floor TFC at month 18, cut free period to 4 months, and cap expansion pricing with a 5% annual uplift.
approval.reasoning_summary: The approval chain is mechanically clear: AE Manager always first, then RevOps + CFO + Legal in parallel (all three are triggered by rules and agent outputs). The high confidence stems from unambiguous rule firing — discount >25%, ACV in $100K–$500K range, and explicit block-priority outputs from Pricing, ASC 606, and Redline agents. The critical issue is not routing but blockers: this deal violates the absolute gross margin floor (10% actual vs. 25% required), the Redline Agent assigned block priority, and ASC 606 flagged two block_without_approval red flags. The AE cannot submit for approval until: (1) deal economics are restructured per Pricing alternatives, (2) TFC is renegotiated to a minimum month-18 floor with wind-down fee, (3) free period is reduced and/or converted to a fee credit, and (4) expansion pricing lock is capped with annual uplift or scoped to the initial term. None of these are approvable exceptions — they require customer renegotiation. Cycle time is 7 days assuming blockers are resolved and all three parallel approvers complete in 3 days each (CFO and Legal typically 3–5 days, but deal urgency and clear deal structure may accelerate).

text.text: Deal Review: Apollo/Outreach/ZoomInfo Displacement — Tessera Health

fields.text: *Approval Chain:* AE Manager → RevOps + CFO + Legal (parallel) · ~7 business days

text.text:
*What the deal team needs to know:*

• *Hard block — deal cannot ship in current form.* Estimated gross margin of ~10% violates the absolute 25% floor. The Pricing Agent flagged five failed guardrails including two `block_without_approval` triggers (discount 33.3% vs. 30% mid-market threshold; 12-month ramp vs. 3-month limit). AE must restructure before submitting for approval — three alternative structures are on file (Option A: 36-mo at 22% disc., ~40% margin; Option B: 24-mo ramp-to-full at 28% disc., ~30% margin; Option C: unbundle PS at 20% disc., ~39% margin).

• *Four non-standard clauses compound the risk — Legal priority is block.* The 90-day anytime termination-for-convenience clause is the most urgent: combined with 6 months free and $40K waived implementation, Tessera could exit after paying as little as ~$60K against ~$160K in delivered value. Redline counters: TFC floor at month 18 minimum (fallback: month 15) with wind-down fee; free period cut to 4 months; expansion pricing lock capped at 5% annual uplift. All four clauses require Legal negotiation before execution.

• *ASC 606 carries two block_without_approval red flags requiring CFO/auditor pre-clearance.* The 90-day TFC may collapse the enforceable contract term to 90 days, invalidating 24-month ratable recognition. The 2026 expansion pricing lock through 2027 likely constitutes a material right requiring transaction price allocation. Neither can be papered over — CFO and auditor must review contract language before the deal closes. Confidence on ASC 606 analysis is rated low given interacting variable-consideration sources; final determination requires direct CFO engagement.

elements.text: *AE Owner:* Priya Shankar · *AE Manager:* Marcus Reilly · *Close Date:* 2026-05-14 · *Customer Health Score:* 58/100

slack_post.plaintext_fallback:
Deal Review: Apollo/Outreach/ZoomInfo Displacement — Tessera Health (Mid-Market)
ACV $240K | TCV $480K | 33.3% effective discount | ~10% est. gross margin (hard floor: 25%) | Close: 2026-05-14
STATUS: BLOCKED — violates absolute gross margin floor and five pricing guardrails; deal must be restructured before approval submission. Three alternative structures on file.
Approval chain: AE Manager Marcus Reilly → RevOps + CFO + Legal (parallel, ~7 business days). CFO and auditor pre-clearance required for 90-day TFC (may collapse ASC 606 contract term) and expansion pricing lock (material right). Legal block priority on all four non-standard clauses — TFC floor, free period reduction, and expansion lock cap are must-fix before execution.
AE Owner: Priya Shankar | review filed by Kiln · agent-driven

ae_email_draft.subject: [Tessera Health] Deal review complete — hard blocks must be resolved before submission
ae_email_draft.body_markdown:
Priya, the deal review is done and this deal **cannot be submitted for approval in its current form** — it hits a hard margin floor (estimated ~10% gross margin vs. the 25% absolute minimum) and carries three block-priority redlines that need to be renegotiated with Tessera before anything else moves.

**Your next steps — in order:**

1. **Restructure the economics.** Pick one of the three Pricing Agent alternatives: Option A (36-month, 22% discount, ~40% margin), Option B (24-month ramp-to-full with Year-2 escalator, ~30% margin), or Option C (unbundle $30K PS, 20% subscription discount, ~39% margin). The current 6-month free period is the single biggest margin killer — it must be eliminated or replaced with a fee credit.
2. **Renegotiate the TFC clause.** The 90-day anytime termination-for-convenience right is a hard block legally and under ASC 606 (it may collapse the enforceable term to 90 days). Counter-proposal: TFC window opens no earlier than month 18, with 90-day notice and a 3-month wind-down fee. Fallback: month 15 floor with a 2-month wind-down fee.
3. **Cap the expansion pricing lock.** The 2026 pricing lock through Dec 2027 likely creates a material right under ASC 606 requiring CFO/auditor pre-clearance. Counter: apply a 5% annual uplift cap and scope the lock to the initial contract term only (not an arbitrary calendar date).

Once those three are addressed, you'll need to submit a completed Pricing Exception Form with competitive context before entering the approval queue. The chain is: Marcus (Step 1) → RevOps + CFO + Legal in parallel (Step 2, all with veto). Expect **7 business days** once submitted — with a May 14 close date, you have no time to spare.

customer_email_draft.subject: Tessera Health × Clay — Revised structure for your review
customer_email_draft.body_markdown:
Thank you for the detailed conversations this week — we understand consolidating Apollo, Outreach, and ZoomInfo into a single platform is a meaningful operational and financial decision, and we want to make this work within your 10-day window.

On the core subscription pricing, we are holding the 35% discount at $240K ACV; however, we need to restructure two elements of the deal package to get internal approvals across the finish line in time. First, on the free period: we can offer four months of free access (months 1–4) rather than six, with the paid term commencing month 5. In lieu of the remaining two free months, we will apply up to $20,000 as an invoice credit against months 5–6 — the net economic impact to Tessera in Year 1 is materially the same, and this structure resolves a revenue-recognition constraint on our end that would otherwise delay CFO sign-off. Second, on the termination-for-convenience right: we cannot accept an unrestricted anytime out on a 24-month deal at this discount depth, but we are prepared to offer a TFC window that opens at month 18, with 90 days' written notice and a wind-down fee equal to three months of the then-current monthly fee — this gives your CRO a genuine exit path after the platform has been fully deployed and measured. On expansion pricing, we will lock your contracted per-unit rate for any seat expansions through the end of the initial term, subject to a 5% annual uplift cap on each contract anniversary; this is the structure we can defend internally and that protects Tessera against meaningful price increases during the relationship. Implementation services remain waived as proposed, up to $20,000 of included onboarding credit, with additional scope priced separately if needed. The clear next step is a 30-minute call with you and your RevOps lead before end of week so we can align on the revised term sheet and submit for parallel CFO and Legal approval — given our 7-day internal cycle, we need to be in motion by May 8 to meet your May 14 decision date.

sections.heading: Headline
sections.content_markdown: Tessera Health (1,200-employee Healthtech, Series C, ~$95M ARR) seeks to consolidate a $320K/yr Apollo + Outreach + ZoomInfo stack onto Clay in a 10-day, three-vendor bake-off. AE proposes a 24-month deal at $240K ACV ($480K TCV) with a 35% discount, 6-month free period, waived $40K implementation, a 90-day anytime termination-for-convenience (TFC) right, and 2026-price expansion lock through end of 2027.

sections.heading: Pricing Summary
sections.content_markdown:
| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| List price | $360,000 ACV |
| Proposed price | $240,000 ACV |
| Stated discount | 35% |
| Effective discount (incl. free period + waived impl.) | **~47% blended** |
| Estimated gross margin | **~10%** |
| Gross margin floor (absolute) | 25% |

**Deal fails every active pricing guardrail simultaneously.** The 10% margin is a hard block — deal cannot ship in current form.

**Alternative structures from Pricing Agent:**
- **Option A** — 36-month commit, 22% discount, drop free period → ~40% margin, $280,800 ACV
- **Option B** — 24-month true ramp (no free period) + 8% Year-2 escalator, 28% discount → ~30% margin, $260,000 ACV
- **Option C** — Unbundle PS at $30K, 20% subscription discount, 60-day free period → ~39% margin, $288K ACV + $30K PS

sections.heading: Risk Findings
sections.content_markdown:
**🔴 Hard Blocks (cannot ship without resolution)**

1. **Gross margin absolute floor breached** — ~10% actual vs. 25% floor. Pricing Agent: hard block. Deal must be restructured before submission.
2. **90-day anytime TFC clause** — Combined with 6-month free period, Tessera could exit at month 9 having paid ~$60K against ~$160K value already delivered. Redline: block. ASC 606: may collapse enforceable contract term to 90 days, invalidating 24-month ratable revenue recognition — requires CFO/auditor pre-clearance.
3. **Expansion pricing lock through Dec 2027** — Decoupled from contract anniversary; at 35% existing discount, expansion units could be priced at 65% of a stale list. ASC 606: likely constitutes a material right requiring transaction price allocation and revenue deferral — CFO/auditor assessment required. Redline: block.
4. **12-month ramp exceeds 3-month mid-market limit** — Triggers mandatory Finance approval for ASC 606 and revenue recognition reasons.

**🟡 Warnings**

5. **6-month free period + waived $40K implementation** — Combined concessions exceed 50% of ACV; must be reflected as discount in transaction price allocation across full 24-month period, not booked as Year-2 catch-up.
6. **Customer health score: 58** — Below average at signing; elevates TFC invocation risk from theoretical to real.
7. **Exa signals are noise** — All external signals match different Tessera entities (Therapeutics, P&C insurance); competitive leverage assessment relies solely on AE-reported bake-off dynamics. No independent corroboration.
8. **35% discount distorts SSP benchmarks** for future deal modeling.

sections.heading: Approval Routing
sections.content_markdown:
| Step | Approver | Veto? | Trigger |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | AE Manager — Marcus Reilly | No | Standard default |
| 2 (parallel) | RevOps | No | ACV $100K–$500K |
| 2 (parallel) | **CFO** | **Yes** | Discount >25%, margin floor breach (block_without_approval), ramp >3 months |
| 2 (parallel) | **Legal** | **Yes** | 4 non-standard clauses, Redline overall priority = block |

**Estimated cycle time: 7 business days** — *but blockers must be resolved before the chain can even start. Close date is May 14 (9 days away). Timeline is extremely tight.*

**Pre-submission requirements:** (1) Restructure deal economics per a Pricing Agent alternative; (2) renegotiate TFC to floor at month 15–18 minimum with wind-down fee; (3) cut free period to ≤4 months or convert to fee credit; (4) add 5% annual uplift cap to expansion lock; (5) submit completed Pricing Exception Form.

sections.heading: Recommendation
sections.content_markdown:
**🔴 HOLD — do not submit for approval in current form.**

This deal violates the absolute gross margin floor (10% vs. 25% required), triggers Redline block priority on three clauses, and contains two ASC 606 block_without_approval red flags; AE must renegotiate TFC (floor at month 18), reduce the free period (max 4 months), cap the expansion pricing lock with a 5% annual uplift, and restructure economics to Option A, B, or C before this deal is approvable — even then, CFO and Legal both hold veto rights and the 9-day close window is at serious risk.

comms.reasoning_summary: Calibrated customer email to firm tone given the deal context. AE email leads with verdict (ship-with-conditions (blockers must clear first)) and a numbered next-step list. One-pager and Slack post both headline the recommendation and surface 4 flagged redlines (4 countered in customer reply). Approval routing summary: ~7 business days through 4-step chain.

synthesis: Pricing is a hard block in current form: the 33.3% discount, 12-month ramp, and ~10% estimated gross margin breach the 25% absolute floor simultaneously, with the 6-month free period and waived $40K implementation compounding margin erosion. ASC 606 treatment is materially at risk because the 90-day termination-for-convenience right may shorten the enforceable term and invalidate ratable 24-month recognition, and a material right allocation for the 2027 expansion lock is likely required. Redline assigns overall block priority — the stack of 35% discount, 6 months free, waived services, and anytime TFC means Tessera could exit having paid ~$60K against ~$160K in delivered value, so the TFC is the single must-fix before this deal moves. Approval routes AE Manager → RevOps + CFO + Legal in parallel (~7 business days), but the AE cannot submit until economics are restructured, TFC is floored at month 18 with a wind-down fee, the free period is reduced or converted to a credit, and the expansion lock is capped. Call: hold and re-scope — communicate to Tessera in a firm tone with a numbered conditions list; none of the four blockers are approvable as exceptions and all require customer renegotiation.
